title: CHART: Billions wiped of mining stocks as gold, silver, copper prices plummet
summary: Stock losses for world’s biggest mining companies near 30% since war’s start as copper enters bear market, silver falls 40% from high and gold suffers worst week in decades. Gold futures in New York fell by $225 an ounce from opening levels to last trade at $4,492 an ounce by late afternoon, a 3.5% decline on the day Friday and more than 11% for the week . As usual silver’s swings were wilder, with the precious metal exchanging hands for $67.81 in after hours trade, a 6.9% drop from the start of trading. Copper ended the day down 4.0% and was last worth 5.30 per pound ($11,690 a tonne), down 7.4% for the week.
